title: Exact and Approximate Nearest-Neighbor Indexes · Program Brief: Rag Exact Vector Baseline
summary: An HNSW index is called exact, its recall is measured against itself, tenant filters are applied after top-k, and memory or build cost is absent from the decision. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the exact vector baseline increment in ANN recall, latency, and resource experiment. Learners progress through pr…
objectives: Build an exact flat-search oracle on a bounded corpus for correctness, recall comparison, and changed-filter tests.
Plan dual encoding, reindexing, compatibility, canaries, cache invalidation, rollback, and old-vector deletion across model changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Rag Exact Vector Baseline, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to ANN recall, latency, and resource experiment.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-ann-indexes-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-ann-indexes-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_ann_indexes_4(vectors: int = 10000, dimensions: int = 384, exact_recall: float = 0.96, latency_ms: float = 18.0, memory_mb: int = 96, filtered: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if vectors <= 0 or dimensions <= 0:
        return False, 0.0, "index-shape"
    if not 0.0 <= exact_recall <= 1.0:
        return False, exact_recall, "recall-range"
    if latency_ms <= 0.0 or memory_mb <= 0:
        return False, latency_ms, "resource-measurement"
    if not filtered:
        return False, exact_recall, "filter-recall"
    utility = exact_recall / latency_ms
    assert utility > 0.0
    return True, round(utility, 5), "ann-tradeoff"

# Evidence: rag-exact-vector-baseline-17kpm9

Competency probe: Build an exact flat-search oracle on a bounded corpus for correctness, recall comparison, and changed-filter tests.

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Exact Vector Baseline model · Theory Rag Exact Vector Baseline, keep Rag Exact Vector Baseline connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Build an exact flat-search oracle on a bounded corpus for correctness, recall comparison, and changed-filter tests.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Exact Vector Baseline model · Theory Rag Exact Vector Baseline, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Exact Vector Baseline precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Exact Vector Baseline model · Theory Rag Exact Vector Baseline, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 1950 during a multilingual support escalation. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare exact, HNSW, IVF, and quantized indexes with recall, latency, memory, and filter evidence; keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects; then remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Build an exact flat-search oracle on a bounded corpus for correctness, recall comparison, and changed-filter tests.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Exact Vector Baseline model · Theory Rag Exact Vector Baseline, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: An approximate index can validate its own recall without an exact baseline.
